element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player chooses between Sypha (magic), Grant (wall-climb), or none; permanent choice

element_introduction_order.element: Path branching (upper vs lower route choice)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Upper route: across lake to front gate; Lower route: tunnels/caverns to cliff climb. Routes converge in castle main hall
Damage values differ between Japanese/Western versions; difficulty scales with stage progression
Lower route contains short branching section
Defeated to progress; lead to convergence in castle
Final stages in main hall; culminates in final boss
Can abandon current companion to pick up another, affecting ending

typical_relief_triggers: Companion abilities (Grant wall-climb for traversal, Sypha magic for range)
Converging paths providing sense of story progression
typical_tension_triggers: Mandatory path choice (Stage 3 branching, Stage 6 secondary split)
Transition from outdoor stages to castle interior
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating difficulty with breathing room between peaks; branching paths create narrative pacing shifts; choice moments act as tension valves

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Stage 2 (companion acquisition) marked as 'optional excursion' - suggests visual/contextual distinction from mandatory path
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hidden features accessible via player name entry (e.g., 'HELP ME' = 10 lives start); second quest difficulty mode hidden behind naming convention

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tutorial-like Stage 1 establishes mechanics; Stage 2 optional companion choice adds modularity
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Path branching at Stage 3 creates two parallel difficulty curves (upper route: lake-to-front gate; lower route: tunnels-to-cliff); Stage 6 secondary branching on lower route; escalating enemy variety and damage
difficulty_curve.late_game: Routes converge in castle main hall (Stages 7+); confined spaces and boss gauntlets; final confrontation with Dracula
notable_spikes: Stage 3 forced path choice (raises stakes narratively)
Stage 6 secondary branching on lower route
Castle interior transition (environment change + enemy density increase)

notable_level_patterns.description: Game splits into two distinct 9-stage routes (upper via lake/front gate, lower via tunnels/cliff) after Stage 3, each with different spatial challenges. Routes reconverge in castle main hall, creating two distinct narrative paths toward same climax.

Each companion unlocks stage-specific traversal or combat advantages (Grant climbs walls/changes jump mid-air; Sypha provides magic range; Alucard fireballs/bat form). Companion choice creates implicit difficulty modulation.
Stage 2 marked as optional but contains mandatory companion selection. Signals that side exploration may yield mechanical upgrades.
Progression from outdoor wilderness (Stages 1-6) to castle interior (Stages 7+) marks tonal and mechanical shift. Confined spaces replace open platforming; enemy density and boss prevalence increase.
